title: Three-tier quick-response code applications for encoded text and counterfeit prevention
author: Ignacio-Cerrato, Pacios, et al.

steps.title: Methodology
steps.instruction: This methodology uses the image combination provided by OpenCV [4,5,6] to create a coding system based on QR codes. The QR Code merged has the information provided in the three QR codes. This is the main key to code the information. On the other hand, we could identify any differences and analyze the luminance between the two separate performances by juxtaposing the theoretical and QR Code obtained merged the three QR codes. First, we build the QR codes [7] shown in Fig. 1 using the RGB coordinates from Eqs. (1), (2), and (3).(1)R¯=[255,0,0](2)G¯=[0,255,0](3)B¯=[0,0,255]Fig. 1Generated QR codes: Red, green and blue.Fig 1

steps.title: Methodology
steps.instruction: Then, we apply the formula provided by the Documentation to obtain the theoretical color values from the QR codes.(4)C¯=αrxR¯+αgxG¯+αbxB¯

steps.title: Methodology
steps.instruction: Being α determined by us to mix a certain percentage of each color. This α controls the chromaticity in each channel and it cannot be greater than 1. In this case the α are the following:(5)αr=0.4(6)αg=0.3(7)αb=0.3
